name: Dehesa de la Calera, Monasterio de Tentudía y Sierra de Aguafría – circular desde la Ermita de San Isidro
region_intro: Mountain biking around Monesterio, located in the province of Badajoz, offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Tentudía and Sierra Morena. The region is characterized by traditional dehesas, open woodlands of oak trees, and significant elevation changes. Riders can explore trails that incorporate the historic Vía de la Plata, an ancient Roman road, and ascend to peaks like Pico Tentudía. The varied terrain provides a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Monesterio The most popular mountain bike trail is Dehesa de la Calera, Monasterio de Tentudía y Sierra de Aguafría – circular desde la Ermita de San Isidro, a 36.8 miles (59.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses dehesas and passes the Monasterio de Tentudía. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Santa Olalla Castle – View of Minas de Teuler loop from Alto del Castillo, a moderate 37.3 miles (60.0 km) path. This route offers views of Santa Olalla Castle and the Minas de Teuler. Local mountain bikers also love the Cerro de Tentudia – Monastery of Tentudía loop from Monesterio, a 27.8 miles (44.8 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Tentudía, often completed in about 4 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Monesterio is defined by the mountainous Sierra de Tentudía, expansive dehesas, and sections of the historic Vía de la Plata. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Monesterio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Monesterio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Monesterio?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails available around Monesterio, catering to various skill levels. The komoot community has recorded 24 moderate and 20 difficult routes in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Monesterio?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Monesterio, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for riding, though some trails might be affected by rain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Monesterio?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, Monesterio's diverse terrain includes sections of the Vía de la Plata and dehesa landscapes that may offer easier, less technical riding suitable for families. It's recommended to check individual route details for elevation and terrain to ensure suitability.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Monesterio?
A: Mountain bike trails in Monesterio feature varied terrain, from the rugged ascents and descents of the Sierra de Tentudía and Sierra Morena to the more open, rolling landscapes of the dehesas. You'll encounter a mix of dirt tracks, gravel paths, and potentially some rocky sections, often incorporating parts of the historic Vía de la Plata.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that include the historic Vía de la Plata?
A: Yes, Monesterio is situated along the historic Vía de la Plata, an ancient Roman road. Many mountain bike routes in the area incorporate sections of this route, offering a unique blend of natural beauty and historical exploration. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032473456/dehesa-de-la-calera-monasterio-de-tentudia-y-sierra-de-aguafria-circular-desde-la-ermita-de-san-isidro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de la Calera, Monasterio de Tentudía y Sierra de Aguafría – circular desde la Ermita de San Isidro&lt;/a&gt; traverses dehesas and passes the Monasterio de Tentudía, which is part of the historical context of the region.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or views I can see while mountain biking near Monesterio?
A: Many routes offer stunning views, particularly those ascending to Pico Tentudía, the highest point in Badajoz. You can also visit the historic &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monasterio_de_Tentud%C3%ADa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Tentudía&lt;/a&gt;, a 13th-century church-fortress with panoramic vistas. Other attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Towers&lt;/a&gt; and the distinctive dehesa landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Monesterio are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121775" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Tentudia – Monastery of Tentudía loop from Monesterio&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Sierra de Tentudía.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Monesterio?
A: The mountain bike trails in Monesterio are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the challenging climbs, and the opportunity to explore historical sites like the Monasterio de Tentudía.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Monesterio offers 20 difficult mountain bike routes, many with significant elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31463047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Aracena – Arroyomolinos de León loop from Monesterio&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 52.5 km route with over 1,200 meters of ascent, providing a demanding experience for advanced riders.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in Monesterio?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not always explicitly listed, routes often start from or pass through Monesterio town or other small villages. It's generally possible to find parking in these areas, especially near local amenities or designated public parking spots. Always check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Monesterio?
A: Many outdoor trails in Spain, especially in natural parks or rural areas like the dehesas around Monesterio, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through livestock areas or protected natural spaces, and to check specific regulations for any particular route or natural park you plan to visit.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of castles?
A: Yes, you can find routes that provide views of historical castles. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Olalla Castle – View of Minas de Teuler loop from Alto del Castillo&lt;/a&gt; offers views of Santa Olalla Castle. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3754437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Towers&lt;/a&gt; is another historical site in the vicinity that can be explored.
tips: Typical and beautiful Extremaduran landscape that we find in this section of the Vía de la Plata that runs through La Dehesa de Calera de León, a beautiful path for lovers of BTT or gravel among trees.
Nice views of the Tentudía reservoir rolling next to it along a wide dirt road.

We are in the beautiful natural environment that surrounds the Monastery of Tentudía, where the beautiful Mudejar cloister stands out especially.

The views from the top of Alto de Tentudía are impressive, offering a panoramic view of the region and its landscapes of meadows and mountains typical of the south of Badajoz.

According to legend, in the 13th century during a battle against the Arabs, the master of the Order of Santiago, Pelay Pérez Correa, cried out to the Virgin shouting: "Holy Mary, stop your day" when he saw that night was approaching and the victory was not assured. It is said that the sun stopped on the horizon allowing the Christian troops to win. As thanks, the master ordered the construction of a temple dedicated to Santa María de Tentudía.

At the end of the visit we can go to the bar where we can eat or drink while enjoying the scenery.

waypoints.name: La Dehesa de Calera de León on the Vía de la Plata
